steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects two ideas. Before the blank: Cuba still speaks Spanish — a strong, living colonial legacy. After the blank: Sardinia barely speaks Spanish — a colonial tie so weak it has almost vanished. The passage is moving from a strong-legacy example to a weak-legacy example. That requires a contrast transition.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Sometimes,': Signals frequency or occasional occurrence, not a contrast between two different cases. ✗
  • Choice B — 'On the other hand,': Directly signals contrast between two alternatives. Cuba's strong legacy is set against Sardinia's near-absent one. ✓
  • Choice C — 'At the same time,': Implies the two things happen simultaneously or coexist in harmony. The passage is highlighting an opposition, not a simultaneity. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In addition,': Adds a similar or supporting point. The Sardinia example is not additional support for Cuba; it is the opposite case. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
